title: Merchant-History Amount Mismatch with Clean Behavioral Profile
narrative: Fraudster operates a compromised card at a legitimate merchant account where the cardholder has established transaction history, but attempts amounts significantly outside the cardholder's prior spending pattern at that specific merchant. V307 engineered feature encodes the amount-vs-merchant-history deviation that raw amount fields cannot capture because the transaction value itself falls within the card's overall spending range. The fraud is invisible in behavioral signals: amount appears normal for the card, device fingerprint may show prior legitimate use, email and address match issuer records. Only the merchant-specific spending anomaly encoded in V307 reveals the attack. This pattern exploits merchant familiarity to bypass behavioral velocity checks while the fraudster tests higher-value purchases than the cardholder's established merchant relationship would support.
indicators: V307 > 1.8 (p85), elevated merchant-specific amount-history deviation score
card1_amt_zscore between -1.2 and +1.2, amount within cardholder's normal range
C1 < 45 (p50), card-counter aggregation showing established but not excessive card activity
V258 < 120 (p40), engineered transaction-sequence feature in normal range masking merchant anomaly
D1 > 0 indicating prior device-card pairing exists
V320 between 0.3 and 0.8 (mid-range), identity-verification composite not flagging mismatch
indicator_explanations: Amount is significantly outside the cardholder's historical spending pattern at this specific merchant
Transaction value fits the card's overall spending baseline across all merchants
Card shows moderate established usage history consistent with legitimate account activity
Transaction sequence timing and pattern appear normal for this cardholder profile
The device has been used with this card before in prior transactions
Identity verification elements show moderate consistency not triggering address or email flags
summary: Card used at familiar merchant but for amounts inconsistent with that specific merchant relationship, detected only through merchant-history comparison features while all raw behavioral signals remain clean.
reasoning: Fraudsters leverage compromised cards at merchants where legitimate history exists to bypass velocity rules, but request amounts outside the cardholder's established spending pattern at that merchant. V307 encodes this merchant-specific deviation that aggregate card-level amount statistics cannot detect.
